programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Young Artist with Caregiver
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art mediums and techniques, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Caregiver
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. A caregiver is required to actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4 - Small Group

programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. programs.name: Ultra Swim 4 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.description: Would your little one enjoy splashing around and getting comfortable in the water at their own pace? This 15-minute activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Wood Working
programs.description: Working with wood is a wonderful way to engage your mind and use your hands. From measuring and designing to cutting and shaping, there are many skills to gain. In this introductory activity, you will learn how to safely use tools and machinery to create your own woodworking project.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Zumba®
programs.description: Latin dance and music inspire this fun and energetic cardio workout. Using high and low-impact steps and aerobic movements, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Adapted Aquatic Games and Skills
programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational (Women)
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise?  With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Want to liven up the kitchen table? Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Functional Fit
programs.description: This conditioning workout is designed to help you build strength to support day-to-day activities. At your own pace, you will practice basic movements like pushing, pulling, bending and squatting to improve your overall functional fitness.

programs.name: Gentle Fit
programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Golf: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational golf. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Mobility and Strength: Chair
programs.description: Improving your mobility and strength can help make day-to-day activities easier.  With a focus on balance and coordination you will use a chair or your walker to complete basic movements in a safe and engaging environment.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Qigong
programs.description: Take part in a series of mind and body exercises to improve your flexibility, strength and posture. With focused intention and relaxed movement, you will learn how to integrate breathing and meditation into your wellness routine.

programs.name: Recreation Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Recreation Discovery with Caregiver
programs.description: Embark on a recreational discovery with a focus on developing skills through creative, active and interactive activities. Programming will include storytelling, theme-based games, crafts, music and physical activity, while having fun with new friends. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Science: Building Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Keep little hands busy with building. Stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play using different materials and tools helps explore how to construct objects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overallendurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Wood Working
programs.description: Working with wood is a wonderful way to engage your mind and use your hands. From measuring and designing to cutting and shaping, there are many skills to gain. In this introductory activity, you will learn how to safely use tools and machinery to create your own woodworking project.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Zumba®
programs.description: Latin dance and music inspire this fun and energetic cardio workout. Using high and low-impact steps and aerobic movements, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Adapted Aquatic Games and Skills
programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational (Women)
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction. This activity is for all women.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
